mnemonics: DEADLOCK D's: Detect, Prevent, Avoid, Ignore (Ostrich), Recover, Don't (Circular Wait)
keyPoints: Deadlock occurs when four conditions hold simultaneously: mutual exclusion, hold and wait, no preemption, and circular wait.
The main strategies are: prevention (designing system to avoid one condition), avoidance (using algorithms like Banker's to ensure safe state), detection and recovery.
The Ostrich Algorithm is a strategy of ignoring the problem, assuming deadlocks are rare and the cost of prevention is too high.
commonMistakes: Confusing deadlock prevention with deadlock avoidance. Prevention removes one of the four conditions, while avoidance uses algorithms to grant resources only when it leads to a safe state.
Assuming that a resource allocation graph with a cycle always means a deadlock is present. This is only true if each resource has a single instance.
